skill.name: pytest-mastery
skill.description: Guides Python developers through pytest workflows with uv, including test execution, fixtures, parametrization, coverage, FastAPI testing, and failure debugging.
skill.summary: Build, run, and debug pytest suites for Python and FastAPI projects using uv.
skill.author: AbdullahMalik17

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found that most are Markdown examples or benign test documentation. The only confirmed executable behavior is a helper script that runs pytest through uv without a shell, which is expected for this skill and presents low residual risk.

low_findings.title: Controlled pytest subprocess runner
low_findings.description: Verdict: TRUE_POSITIVE for external command execution, but low severity. The script builds an argument list for uv run pytest and calls subprocess.call without shell=True, reducing command injection risk. Additional pytest arguments are user supplied by design, so users should run it only in trusted projects. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The subprocess call and argument flow are directly visible, and the command purpose matches the pytest helper script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call and argument flow are directly visible, and the command purpose matches the pytest helper script.

low_findings.title: Markdown command examples misclassified as shell execution
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for Ruby or shell backtick execution. The cited SKILL.md and FastAPI reference locations are Markdown code fences, inline code spans, option tables, and pytest examples, not executable skill code. Confidence: 0.94. Confidence reasoning: The cited files are documentation, and the line context shows fenced examples rather than runtime execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files are documentation, and the line context shows fenced examples rather than runtime execution paths.

low_findings.title: Test-only URL and database examples are benign
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for network and credential access. The hardcoded URL is http://test used as an httpx ASGI test base URL, and the SQLite value is a local test database string in documentation. No evidence found that the skill exfiltrates secrets, reads environment variables, or connects to an external service. Confidence: 0.91. Confidence reasoning: The relevant lines are inside FastAPI testing examples and show local test fixtures rather than production credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant lines are inside FastAPI testing examples and show local test fixtures rather than production credentials.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography alerts lack supporting code evidence
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for weak cryptographic algorithm usage. The cited lines contain YAML description text, pytest option tables, dependency override examples, factory test data, and mode names. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic API calls in the reviewed files. Confidence: 0.90. Confidence reasoning: The cited contexts do not contain cryptographic operations, only documentation terms and ordinary Python data structures.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited contexts do not contain cryptographic operations, only documentation terms and ordinary Python data structures.

low_findings.title: Critical heuristic combination dismissed after context review
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE for dangerous combination of code execution, network, and credential access. The network and database indicators are test documentation, while the only executable command is a local pytest runner. No evidence found of obfuscation, prompt injection, credential harvesting, or data exfiltration. Confidence: 0.88. Confidence reasoning: The component alerts were reviewed in context and do not combine into a malicious execution path.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The component alerts were reviewed in context and do not combine into a malicious execution path.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.call invokes uv run pytest
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper script executes uv run pytest with a list of arguments. This is an expected capability for a testing skill, but it remains an external command pattern. Confidence: 0.86. Confidence reasoning: The call target and arguments are visible, and shell execution is not enabled.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The call target and arguments are visible, and shell execution is not enabled.

content.value_statement: Python teams need repeatable tests that are easy to run, debug, and extend. This skill gives practical pytest and uv guidance for unit tests, coverage, fixtures, and FastAPI test patterns.
actual_capabilities: Shows uv commands for running all tests, specific files, named tests, markers, and coverage reports.
Explains pytest fixture patterns, fixture scopes, shared conftest.py fixtures, and parametrized tests.
Provides pyproject.toml examples for pytest discovery, markers, addopts, and coverage settings.
Documents FastAPI testing patterns with TestClient, async httpx clients, dependency overrides, and auth tests.
Includes a helper script for common pytest modes such as quick, full, coverage, failed, and watch.
limitations: Does not inspect the user project automatically before giving test guidance.
Does not guarantee that generated tests match every framework version or plugin combination.
The helper script runs local pytest commands and should be used only in trusted repositories.
FastAPI examples are templates and may need changes for each application structure.

use_cases.title: Add unit tests to a Python package
use_cases.description: Create pytest files, fixtures, parametrized checks, and focused commands for a standard Python project.

use_cases.title: Test FastAPI endpoints
use_cases.description: Set up clients, dependency overrides, database fixtures, authentication headers, and response assertions.

use_cases.title: Improve test execution in CI
use_cases.description: Use markers, coverage thresholds, short tracebacks, and repeatable uv commands for faster feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Run my pytest suite
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pytest-mastery to recommend the uv pytest command for my Python project and explain when to use verbose output.

prompt_templates.title: Write fixture-based tests
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pytest-mastery to design pytest fixtures and parametrized tests for this module. Keep setup reusable and readable.

prompt_templates.title: Add FastAPI endpoint coverage
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pytest-mastery to create a FastAPI testing plan with TestClient, dependency overrides, authentication, and database fixtures.

prompt_templates.title: Optimize pytest configuration
prompt_templates.prompt: Use pytest-mastery to review my pytest and coverage configuration. Suggest markers, addopts, coverage reports, and CI-friendly commands.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to run only integration tests with concise tracebacks.
output_examples.output: The response recommends a uv pytest command using a marker expression and short traceback output, then explains where to define the marker.

output_examples.input: A FastAPI team needs tests for authenticated endpoints.
output_examples.output: The response outlines client fixtures, a mocked current user dependency, bearer headers, and assertions for successful and failed access.

output_examples.input: A project wants coverage gates in local runs and CI.
output_examples.output: The response proposes pytest-cov options, terminal and HTML reports, a minimum threshold, and matching pyproject settings.

best_practices: Keep fixtures small, named clearly, and scoped only as broadly as needed.
Use parametrization for repeated logic instead of copying similar test functions.
Separate fast unit tests from slower integration tests with explicit pytest markers.
anti_patterns: Do not share mutable fixture state across unrelated tests unless isolation is guaranteed.
Do not hide failing tests with broad skip or xfail markers without a clear reason.
Do not run the helper script in untrusted repositories or with unknown pytest plugins.

faq.question: Does this skill require uv?
faq.answer: The examples use uv commands. The pytest concepts still apply if your project uses another Python package manager.

faq.question: Can it help with FastAPI tests?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes TestClient setup, async httpx clients, dependency overrides, auth tests, and database fixture examples.

faq.question: Does it generate tests automatically?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and templates for tests. The final tests should be adapted to the application code.

faq.question: Can it configure coverage reports?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents pytest-cov commands and pyproject.toml settings for terminal, HTML, and XML reports.

faq.question: Is the included script safe?
faq.answer: The script runs uv run pytest without a shell. Use it only in trusted projects because pytest plugins and tests execute local code.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
